===''Pokémon the Series''=== ====History==== ====={{aniseries|RS}}===== [[File:Steven Stone anime AG.png|thumb|left|200px|Steven in {{aniseries|RS}}]] In ''[[AG017|Stairway to Devon]]'', Steven was first mentioned by [[Mr. Stone|his father]], who claimed he would be a good person for {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}} to know. In ''[[AG021|Which Wurmple's Which?]]'', [[Nurse Joy]] informed [[Max]] that Steven was in nearby [[Granite Cave]], so the group began their search for him. He then debuted in ''[[AG022|A Hole Lotta Trouble]]''. Unlike his game counterpart, Steven was not mentioned to be or have been the [[Hoenn League]] {{pkmn|Champion}}, but was simply a powerful {{pkmn|Trainer}}. Instead, [[Wallace]] was said to be the Champion later in ''[[Pokémon the Series: Diamond and Pearl]]''. Steven and his {{p|Aron}} were digging in Granite Cave, looking for rare stones. As Ash, his friends, and {{TRT}} fell down a hole, Steven ended his excavation and quickly checked out what caused the crash to occur. Steven first found Max, {{AP|Pikachu}}, and {{MTR}} wandering around the cave. Having been in Granite Cave for several months, Steven escorted Max and the others to a crack in the cave floor. Soon enough they spotted Ash and the others, who were being chased by a horde of agitated Aron. He ordered them to rush over to the nearby waterfall. At the meeting spot, Steven had his own Aron use {{m|Roar}} to calm the agitated horde. He then told the horde that the group were good people and ordered them back to their roosts. After both sides were reunited with each other, Steven found out that Team Rocket were the ones who caused the Aron to get angry. Steven handled Team Rocket on his own by having his {{p|Aggron}} blast them off with a {{m|Hyper Beam}}. Afterwards, Steven found a [[Fire Stone]] with the help of Aron. He decided to lead the group out of Granite Cave and took them to a deserted inlet, an ideal place for Ash to train for his [[rematch]] with [[Brawly]]. Having found a rare stone, Steven boarded his small boat to continue his search for other stones and wished the group luck for the future. =====[[Mega Evolution Specials]]===== [[File:Steven Stone anime XY.png|thumb|230px|Steven in ''[[Pokémon the Series: XY]]'']] Steven reappeared in ''[[XYS02|Mega Evolution Special II]]'', where he battled [[Alain]] using his {{Shiny}} [[Mega Evolution|Mega]] {{TP|Steven|Metagross}}, but the battle was interrupted by [[Lysandre]]. Deciding to cooperate with Alain and company, Steven traveled to their destination, a small temple with two pillars in the center. When Alain and Steven held their [[Key Stone]]s to the monument, a staircase of light appeared to lead them further within the ancient ruin. There, they discovered a similar mechanism that summoned a glowing [[Giant Rock]] from underneath the ruin. After Lysandre and his research team began analyzing the giant stone, a {{DL|Super-ancient Pokémon (XY series)|Rayquaza}} appeared from the sky, Mega Evolved into Mega Rayquaza, and started wreaking havoc on the surrounding area. Alain, Steven, and their Mega Pokémon partners tried to fend off the Legendary Pokémon, but were overwhelmed. [[File:Steven and Alain.png|thumb|250px|left|Steven and Alain]] In this [[series]], Steven was confirmed to be the current Champion of the Hoenn League, indicating that Wallace no longer holds the title. In ''[[XYS03|Mega Evolution Special III]]'', Steven brought Alain and [[Mairin]] to the [[Devon Corporation]] headquarters to analyze the situation of the missing Giant Rock. There, they received a conference call from Lysandre, who informed them that both {{DL|Super-ancient Pokémon (XY series)|Groudon}} and {{DL|Super-ancient Pokémon (XY series)|Kyogre}} have undergone [[Primal Reversion]] and were set to collide in the middle of the ocean. Steven recalled the Primal Reversion myth, and feared that the two Pokémon clashing again could have catastrophic consequences. As a result, he and Alain departed on a plane to check on the situation from the sky, with Mairin secretly stowing away with them. In ''[[XYS04|Mega Evolution Special IV]]'', he, Mairin, and [[Chespie]] returned to [[Kalos]]. They contacted {{an|Professor Sycamore}} regarding Alain as he separated from them. Later, they arrived at [[Lysandre Labs]] with Lysandre and his secretary waiting for them. While Lysandre's secretary escorted Mairin to watch Alain battle ten Trainers, Steven and Lysandre discussed the Giant Rock. Lysandre showed Steven the Giant Rock in another room and explained that it is kept in a high-security tank to prevent energy from leaking out, as well as bringing up the fact that the [[super-ancient Pokémon]] fought in [[Hoenn]] previously. Steven was then seen watching the battle between Alain and [[Malva]]. After the battle, Steven arrived to where Chespie was lying on a bed, unconscious from absorbing too much energy from an experiment. ====={{aniseries|XY}}===== [[File:Steven and Sawyer.png|thumb|250px|Steven and Sawyer]] Steven reappeared in the main series in a flashback in ''[[XY075|Rivals: Today and Tomorrow!]]'', where he met [[Sawyer]]. He also appeared in another flashback in ''[[XY133|Coming Apart at the Dreams!]]''. From ''[[XY134|The Right Hero for the Right Job!]]'' to ''[[XY136|Forming a More Perfect Union!]]'', Steven joined the battle against [[Team Flare]]. He eventually battled alongside Ash, Alain, [[Diantha]], and all of Kalos's [[Gym Leader]]s during the joint effort to stop the [[Giant Rock]]. In ''[[XY138|The First Day of the Rest of Your Life!]]'', Sawyer revealed that he received his [[Key Stone]] from Steven. Later, in ''[[XY140|Till We Compete Again!]]'', Steven and Sawyer were shown at Lysandre Labs, studying the Giant Rock that was recently held there. He proceeded to give Sawyer some information, which Sawyer promptly wrote down in his notebook. ====={{aniseries|JN}}===== [[File:Steven Stone JN.png|250px|thumb|left|Steven in ''[[Pokémon Journeys: The Series]]'']] Steven appeared in a fantasy in ''[[JN105|Reuniting for the First Time!]]'', when {{Ash}} mentioned him to [[Wallace]]. In ''[[JN109|Aim For The Eight!]]'', Steven was revealed to be in the Master Class of the [[World Coronation Series]], being ranked third. Steven appeared in a fantasy in ''[[JN114|Friends, Rivals, Lend Me Your Spirit!]]'', when [[Goh]] mentioned him to [[Paul]]. [[File:Ash and Steven.png|thumb|250px|Steven and Ash]] In ''[[JN115|Curtain Up! Fight the Fights!]]'', Steven participated in the [[Masters Eight Tournament]], along with the rest of the Masters Eight, reuniting with Ash, Alain, and Diantha, and meeting {{an|Iris}} and the other regional Champions. When it was revealed that Ash would be his first opponent, Steven revealed that he would finally have the chance to battle him. When asked by {{an|Lance}} if he knew Alain, Steven replied that he knew him through the Key Stones excavations and also revealed that he hoped Alain would not be overwhelmed by {{an|Leon}} in the very first match of the tournament. After watching the other battles in the [[JN116|following]] [[JN117|episodes]], Steven and Ash began their battle. In ''[[JN118|Battling as Hard as Stone!]]'', Steven was able to hold his own against Ash, but was defeated by the [[Alola]] Champion, making him the fourth person to be eliminated from the tournament. Following his loss, Steven wished Ash good luck for the remainder of the competition and returned to Hoenn. In ''[[JN129|A Flood of Torrential Gains!]]'', Steven watched Ash's match against Leon. {{-}} ====Character==== Similar to his game counterpart, Steven is reserved and interested in collecting [[Evolution stone|stones]]. During ''[[Pokémon the Series: Ruby and Sapphire]]'', he did care for the {{p|Aron}} in the [[Granite Cave]] and attempted to stop a herd of them from chasing {{Ash}}, {{ashfr}}, and {{TRT}}. He battled Team Rocket after discovering that they were the ones who made the Aron horde angry. During {{aniseries|XY}}, he became more open to Trainers, such as [[Alain]] and [[Mairin]]. He is seen as a sort of mentor figure by [[Sawyer]], whom Steven gave a [[Key Stone]] and [[Sceptilite]]. His usual calm, and a bit playful demeanor is constantly reflected throughout the series, such as during his battles against Alain and [[Mable]]; nevertheless, he can be serious when the situation demands. In a particular instance, he was not greatly disappointed or enraged after learning [[Lysandre]]'s true intentions, displaying his happy-go-lucky personality. Being the gentle and playful person that he is, he was not worried when he and his {{TP|Steven|Metagross}} were outnumbered by Mable and {{tc|Team Flare Grunt}}s during their battle, also reflecting his strong bond and great confidence in his Pokémon. He tends to appreciate others for their efforts, and has a great sense of honor for fellow [[Pokémon Champion|Champion]]s, as displayed during his interactions with [[Diantha]], {{an|Cynthia}}, and {{an|Leon}}. ====Pokémon==== {{MissingInfo|2|enva|java}} This listing is of Steven's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Steven |pkmn=Aron |type1=Steel |type2=Rock |img=Steven Aron.png |epnum=AG022 |epname=A Hole Lotta Trouble |vajp=Natsuki Yoshihara |vaen=Darren Dunstan |desc={{p|Aron}} was always seen outside its {{i|Poké Ball}} in its debut appearance, helping Steven during his search for [[Evolution stone|stones]] in [[Granite Cave]]. After {{TRT}}, along with {{Ash}} and {{ashfr}} fell into one of the Rocket trio's pitfall traps, Aron and Steven began to search for the source of the crash. Later Aron Roared at the rampaging wild Aron, helping to settle them down. After the trio were sent blasting off, Aron pointed Steven to a [[Fire Stone]] in a nearby dirt pile. Aron and Steven said their farewells to the group as they headed off into the distance on a sailing boat. Aron briefly reappeared in ''[[XYS02|Mega Evolution Special II]]''. Aron's only known move is {{m|Roar}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Steven |pkmn=Aggron |type1=Steel |type2=Rock |img=Steven Aggron.png |epnum=AG022 |epname=A Hole Lotta Trouble |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Aggron}} was briefly used when {{TRT}} were trying to steal [[Ash's Pikachu]] in [[Granite Cave]]. It sent them blasting off, and was soon recalled back into its {{i|Poké Ball}}. In ''[[JN118|Battling as Hard as Stone!]]'', Steven used Aggron as his second Pokémon in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}. It battled and defeated his {{AP|Dracovish}} before being defeated by {{AP|Gengar}}. Aggron's known moves are {{m|Hyper Beam}}, {{m|Heavy Slam}}, {{m|Smart Strike}}, and {{m|Rock Tomb}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |main=Steven's Metagross |trainer=Steven |pkmn=Metagross |shiny=yes |mega=yes |type1=Steel |type2=Psychic |img=Steven Shiny Metagross.png |cap=Steven's Metagross |img2=Steven Shiny Mega Metagross.png |cap2=Steven's Mega Metagross |epnum=XYS02 |epname=Mega Evolution Special II |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Kenta Miyake |desc=Steven sent out this {{Shiny}} {{p|Metagross}} against [[Alain's Charizard]]. It also fought {{DL|Super-ancient Pokémon (XY series)|Rayquaza}} and assisted in protecting the [[Giant Rock]] against [[Primal Reversion|Primal]] {{DL|Super-ancient Pokémon (XY series)|Groudon}} and Primal {{DL|Super-ancient Pokémon (XY series)|Kyogre}}. It has the ability to [[Mega Evolution|Mega Evolve]] into Mega Metagross.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Steven |pkmn=Cradily |type1=Rock |type2=Grass |img=Steven Cradily.png |epnum=JN118 |epname=Battling as Hard as Stone! |vajp= |vaen=Emily Jenness |desc={{p|Cradily}} is Steven's fourth known Pokémon. Steven used it in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}, where it battled and defeated {{AP|Gengar}} before being defeated by {{AP|Pikachu}}. Cradily's known moves are {{m|Power Whip}}, {{m|Ingrain}}, {{m|Meteor Beam}}, and {{m|Rock Blast}}.}} ====Achievements==== [[File:Steven WCS Rank.png|thumb|250px|Steven's [[World Coronation Series]] rank]] =====Pokémon League===== Steven has competed in the following [[Pokémon League Conference]]s: * [[Hoenn League]] - Champion (prior to ''[[XYS02|Mega Evolution Special II]]'') =====World Coronation Series===== Steven has competed in the [[World Coronation Series]]. His rank and class are as follows: * Rank: 3 (Master Class; ''[[JN109|Aim For The Eight!]]'') * [[Masters Eight Tournament]] - Top 8 (''[[JN118|Battling as Hard as Stone!]]'') ====Artwork==== {| style="margin:auto; text-align:center; {{roundy|20px}} border: 2px solid #{{steel color dark}}; background: #{{steel color}}; font-size:80%;" |- |style="{{roundy|20px}} border:2px solid #{{steel color dark}}; background:#{{steel color light}}"| [[File:Master Class Artwork JN.png|x250px]] | style="{{roundy|20px}} border:2px solid #{{steel color dark}}; background:#{{steel color light}}; width:96px; height:96px"| [[File:Steven XY anime.png|150px]] | style="{{roundy|20px}} border:2px solid #{{steel color dark}}; background:#{{steel color light}}; width:96px; height:96px"| [[File:Steven Stone M8 JN.png|x250px]] | style="{{roundy|20px}} border:2px solid #{{steel color dark}}; background:#{{steel color light}}; width:96px; height:96px"| [[File:Masters 8 AS 2.png|x200px]] | style="{{roundy|20px}} border:2px solid #{{steel color dark}}; background:#{{steel color light}}; width:96px; height:96px"| [[File:JN118 AS.png|x200px]] |- | Artwork from
